title: Not Effective
review_body: I discovered lice eggs in my daughter's hair an ordered this to treat her. I knew it wasn't going to work because the comb would not catch any of the eggs just basically combed her hair. It took me a couple of hours sectioning off tiny sections of hair and sprayed it onto the scalp and massaged it in the sprayed the length of her hair. Long story short, I did the meticulous process just to find more eggs in her hair. I also did the process on my hair after seeing some eggs but still feel itchy. I have super thick, wavy/curly hair and so does my daughter. I tried to buy the cheaper option, and now I need to go back and do the process again with another product. I have seen better combs to scrape the eggs off, so maybe if the comb was better it may have worked.

title: This is total rubbish
review_body: Bought it from reading reviews but it didn\'t work. Put it in my kids hair, waited even longer than stated. Nothing was killed even the eggs were not even killed. The only thing that worked, i mixed conditioner with olive oil put in hair and combed bit by bit using lice comb. Washed hair with fairy liquid to get rid of the oily mess. Shampoo and condition as usual. Repeat every 3days till all the eggs are gone. This is very time consuming but the only way to get rid of lice for good. Don\'t forget to wash hats,coats, hair ties, pillows and beddings. Good luck.

title: Don;t be a NitWit
review_body: Right, in an ideal world there would be no such thing as head lice, but we don\'t live in ideal world do we? When the little buggers make their appearance and decide to throw down roots (pun intended) in your children\'s hair well then we all turn to the products that will hand them an eviction notice in the form of death. Unfortunately this product does not do that. This product just gives a friendly knock on the door but then leaves. So the little buggers are happy out, alive & squatting away! Don\'t waste your money, just get a good lice comb and lather the hair with conditioner, comb strand by strand and repeat this for 3 days in a row, and again a week after that, job done!

title: Waste of money.
review_body: First & last time I have bought a chemical head lice solution. I had high hopes after reading some of the reviews, but despite leaving the product on for longer than suggested, it had no effect. It is only good for use as a standard oil to slow down the lice & for making it easier to comb through the hair. I eventually got rid of the lice & eggs by using my usual method of thorough & repeated combing with a Nitty Gritty comb. Save your money & don\'t try to be lazy, as I was, in seeking a quick fix. I have prevented the lice from returning by spraying the hair daily with tea tree oil diluted in water.

I\'ve since used 4 different intensive hair conditioning treatments and the only thing left to do now is to cut off my hair to shoulder length. title: Not great for curly method girls
review_body: As far as products go this is a decent one - it's a spray so it's easy to apply. It's only on for 15-20 minutes so easy to fit in. It's like oil and coats the hair making it slippy so ideal for using a nit comb. I have yet to find a product that works without combing every single piece of hair with one of those nit combs. Using a hairdryer on the fairly hot setting also helps but you risk burning your child's head. This is meant to kill them all in one but honestly I really don't know. I kept finding them after treatments. A quick go with the hair straightners and using conditioner with demithicone in it is your best bet. And brushing with the comb. Not even a fancy nitty gritty one\... just the normal close pronged ones. My main issue with this is that it is almost impossible to get it out without serious stripping shampoo which is murder on my curly hair. The product is basically a concentrated silicone. Quite nice for my straight haired kids but awful for me.

title: Pretty good
review_body: Works pretty good. I would leave it in overnight or for 24hrs. Then comb and wash. Repeat in 5 days and again in 5 days to be sure. I\'ve had lice 2 times and they are very very hard to get rid of. Kapow is very good too. And leave it in 24hrs. Not 10 minutes. Sleep in it. Put a plastic cover on head and make sure it\'s tight. Use a clip or tie a rubber band on the head cover to make sure it\'s close to scalp and tight. Leaving it on for 24 hrs will kill most if not all live bugs. But don\'t forget to repeat every 5 days at least 3 times. Good Luck. And be patient. No need to clean the whole house lice can only live up to 36 hrs off the head and normally don\'t leave the scalp. But I would wash your bedding everything you treat. I hope this helps. Use any product with dimethicone in it. Or buy pure dimethicone and mix some olive oil in it and you have your own product. Much cheaper to do too.

title: Can irritate eyes.
review_body: Beware with this one - whilst it does seem to get rid of the nits it can cause eye irritation. You also need to cover the floors, particularly if you have wooden floors as the floorboards will be covered and you\'ll be squeaking around for days. I\'d say you really need someone to apply this for you (if you are the unfortunate sufferer with these pests) and make sure you keep your eyes closed and open all the windows afterwards. Having tried a few of these solutions now, including nit combs and conditioner, I\'d say solutions which come in a bottle applied directly to the head are easier to manage. Personally I find this stuff really irritates my eyes for a good day or so after - research the active ingredient - dimethicone - before you buy.

title: "Nits & Eggs all die" was where this product fell 😩

I've had kids at primary school & nursery for the last 17 years, so periodically have these critters visit. Liked the reviews for this and the product claim, so thought I'd give it a go. 1 nit in my youngest's hair which lived through the complete process and came out with combing (which you don't have to do apparently) very much alive. What I've known for a while is still true. The best way is to shower, leave some conditioner in and comb with a nitty gritty until it's clear. Takes longer, but it really is the only thing that works. Now, where do I go for a full refund?

title: Dangerously slippy
review_body: This product like many others which contain Dimethicone can present a serious slip hazard. This one is a spray and if you have a tiled floor like the one in our kitchen and bathroom, you find that even the tiniest amount of it getting on to the floor creates an invisible hyper-slippy sheen. Its not water soluble and so even scrubbing with soap or repeated rubbing really doesn\'t get it off. I have never experienced anything quite so slippy before and just about every member of the family has nearly fallen over on the patches of it which we can\'t get off the floor. Last night my wife nearly cracked her head open as the bathroom mat flew sideways as a tiny droplet of it had got underneath. Be careful out there!

title: Nit-Nuking Wonder Spray
review_body: After our third lice outbreak announcement this school year, I was ready to shave everyone\'s heads. Then I found this spray miracle.One application, followed by the included comb, has sent every nit in our household packing. The best part? It\'s simple enough to use on all the kids without starting a mutiny. My only complaint is that the trigger handle seems to be irresistible to my children, who would rather spray each other than help with the actual lice eradication. If you\'re dealing with nits or have children who bring them home like trading cards, this product is worth its weight in gold. Just remember to hide it when not in use.

title: Kills most lice
review_body: The all in one head lice treatment is an effective way to remove lice, the only downside of this product is the oiliness of the solution, it needs a thorough shampoo to remove properly. Overall it provided relief for an itchy head. Good value for money.

title: Does not kill eggs
review_body: My daughter had a lice outbreak at school and we were short on money so this was the cheapest I could find on short notice. It does kill everything on her head as promised but it does not kill the eggs. It smells ok for what it is but it is not my favorite scent. The comb was a bit of a joke, small plastic and not thin enough to even get lice (dead) that we could physically see on her head after treating. But after buying some metal ones later, we discovered it was much of the same. So we used this, and everything died so we assumed it worked on the eggs, then we saw more lice in her hair. We decided to pay a professional lice person to come out. after about \$250 of her services and buying her treatment that \'kills lice in minutes\' again, a couple weeks later even with continuous use of her product we were finding lice in my daughter\'s hair. She wasn\'t completely useless though, she gave me a great idea after listening to her talk about the lice life cycle. So i had the idea that has kept our house lice free for a while now. I bought a couple boxes of this (Nitwit\'s) since I know it will kill all of the alive lice and for three weeks in a row on Saturday we treated her hair. We didn\'t comb it. Just killed the alive lice, since it takes eggs 7-10 days to hatch and we weren\'t sure how many eggs were laid prior to the first treatment, an extra week worked wonders (3 weeks for us). 4 stars because it doesn\'t kill the eggs like it says and the comb is a joke. I still love the product and will buy again if this happens.

title: Fairly good

title: did the job
review_body: I tried the NitWits all-in-one lice spray for my household, and overall it did the job, but not quite in one go as advertised. The spray is easy to apply and much less messy than traditional treatments, which I really appreciated---especially when dealing with kids. After the first use, it definitely reduced the lice, but it took a couple more treatments (and thorough combing) to fully get rid of everything, including the eggs. Once we stayed consistent, it worked. I also like that it's gentle enough for both adults and children and doesn't have an overwhelming smell. Not a perfect one-and-done solution, but still effective with a bit of patience---solid 4 stars from me.

title: So far so good
review_body: I teach kindergarten and kids too often come in with lice. I keep this on hand and do a treatment when they come in with them. I like how gentle it is on my hair. So far so good.

title: Excellent
review_body: I bought this spray because I needed a fast and effective treatment against lice and nits. I was surprised by how easy it is to apply: the dispenser sprays well, doesn\'t drip, and the smell is tolerable. After the first use, we already saw a significant reduction, and the included comb made removing the nits easy. The best part is that it doesn\'t leave my hair too greasy like some other treatments.

title: Quick & Easy Treatment
review_body: Click to play video Pros: \*\*\*Quick Results\*\*\* The first treatment showed great results with many dead lice found after combing through. The itching stopped almost right away, which was a huge plus. \*\*\*Effective Over Time\*\*\* After the second treatment, I only found baby lice, which I suspect were from eggs that had hatched. This shows the spray does a thorough job, even if follow-up treatments are needed. \*\*\*Gentle Formula\*\*\* The formula is gentle on the scalp, non-toxic, and pesticide-free, which made me feel confident using it, especially on children. Cons: \*\*\*Leaky Bottle\*\*\* The only downside I encountered was that the spray bottle tends to leak a little when I use it, so I always end up with some of the product on my fingers. \*\*\*Small Comb\*\*\* The comb that comes with the spray is quite small and isn\'t very practical for people with long hair. It can be a struggle to use, so I recommend having a better lice comb on hand if your hair is thick or long. Overall, Nitwits All-in-One Spray worked well.

title: Seems to have done the job
review_body: My daughter had a head full of lice again (someone in her class seems to need a treatment too as they keep giving them to her!) Usually I just rigorously comb it twice a day but a bit sick of it this time so thought I\'d give this a try. Although the instructions say combing beforehand isn\'t necessary I thought I\'d do it anyway just to get as many as I could first (there were loads!) I already had a good metal comb which is fortunate because the one they\'re supposed to give you was missing from the box, which is why I\'ve knocked off 1 star. Anyway I applied it all to her head which left her looking like she\'d been doused in oil, although she didn\'t complain about any smell or discomfort. After leaving it on for 20 mins we washed it off and the next day I checked her head and all seemed clear. I do think the pre-combing was a good idea although it may have been just as effective without. Make sure you have a comb provided in the box though as you should have one.

title: Brilliant but need to know\.....
review_body: This product was really easy to use and the smell is quite pleasant and not instantly identifiable like some other treatments. However - unless you have severely dry hair you will not get away with just one shampoo following treatment. The product is oil based and leaves hair extremely greasy so you wouldn\'t want to leave the house like that. I\'ve washed my granddaughters\' twice and they wouldn\'t let me do it a third time. Luckily we\'ll have time to shampoo again before school on Monday. Another point to watch out for: take care not to allow any of the spray onto a hard floor because the oil it contains will make the surface dangerously slippery. These points should definitely be included in the instructions. That said, I woukd definitely buy thus priduct again.

title: when they come with the description \'I like it\' or
review_body: \'I love it\' Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 10 November 2017 Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) It\'s hard to choose how many stars, when they come with the description \'I like it\' or \'I love it\'. I doubt anyone really likes nit solution - the ideal solution would be for our children not to return home and scratch their heads, after gaining some little lodgers from another child. But nits are a fact of life for children, and more than likely they will be exposed to multiple infestations. So this nit solution works in a similar way to a number of others by dehydrating the lice and eggs. You are still meant to comb out the dead lice and eggs, and invariably you may need to repeat the application if a nit has managed to escape your initial treatment. This stuff works though. Hopefully we\'ll have respite from head lice for a while.

title: Nit Kicker
review_body: Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) As parents of 5 and 3 year old monkeys who attend school and nursery, Nits are, sadly, a frequent visitor in our home nowadays. Nitwits has been a go to brand ever since we first saw the tell tale scratching at the nape of the neck. The product is a good one, seems to be fairly effective, two treatments over 7 days seems enough to clear the horrible beasties away until the next time. The texture is like conditioner which makes for easier combing through, a big plus given our youngest has curly ringlets that just love tangling themselves up. There is an odour, I personally really dislike it but it doesn\'t bother my wife or the kids at all so I guess it\'s case of try it to see on that score. Overall, it\'s a good product, does the job.

title: Does the job - not the cheapest
review_body: Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) Every now and then there\'s an outbreak of lice at my daughter\'s school. She has fine curly hair which is a real pain to try to comb out so a good lice solution is crucial. And this one worked fine when combined with careful egg hunting after the treatment. I think it may have helped that we caught the infestation very early on. Either way, with a not-unpleasant fragrance we\'d use it again when and if the need arises.

title: It will kill all head lice
review_body: Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) This is a very similar product to Hedrin spray. It\'s an oily lotion which will need to cover all your head (there\'s enough for 2 applications for long hair). Leave it for 20 minutes and then rinse off. I did find this one easier to completely rinse off than Hedrin. You will still need to comb the hair through with a metal nits comb to remove the dead head lice and their eggs. Make sure you keep checking every 3 days to ensure you got them all, otherwise the infestation will come back.

title: It works!

title: Amazing product! 10/10 recommend
review_body: Wow. I have two kids with infestations right now. One has short thick hair, the other long thin hair. I used \$15 RID lice killing shampoo last week, but it didn't kill eggs. What's the point of that?! I bought This stuff because it kills eggs specifically, so hopefully I won't have to keep treating and washing blankets in another week. The space between teeth in the comb on the NITWIT brand is noticeably smaller than the comb that came with the RID brand. The NITWIT brand comb was able to remove many more eggs and lice than the RID comb. The oily nature of the NITWIT brand was a benefit. It made the comb glide effortlessly without damaging and pulling their hair. I was able to Remove many more eggs in a smaller amount of time because their hair was so slick and easy to part. The lather from the RID brand prevented me from being able to glide the comb through their hair, until after the product was washed out and even then the hair was left dry and tangly. After I washed the NITWIT product out their hair was soft and shiny. I was still able to get the lice comb through their hair easily without complaints from the kids. There was a pleasant smell during and after the treatment. I will update my review in a couple days to see if it was effective, but after using the comb I'm confident this childhood dilemma is OVER!

title: Try it. Worked for me!
review_body: I never write reviews, mostly because it is an entirely subjective exercise. This, however, I made an exception for. Let me give you some context. I am not a worn out parent fighting the good fight against these pesky blood suckers. Dearest Reader, I almost wish I was. No, instead I am a 22 year old university student. And I HAD nits. I caught them on a night out. The last big blow out before final exams. Firstly- I was in denial. I have quite sensitive skin see Dearest Reader, and I had just changed shampoo and conditioner. I put the excess itching down to that. After changing my shampoo and conditioner for the next wash, and I was still wanting to claw my scalp off, I put it down to stress. Final exams see Dearest Reader, and you never know what tricks your body will play on you when you're stressed. It all changed about 3am a week later. I had woken up and my scalp felt like it was on fire. I ordered the metal nit comb from here, and the next day I set to it, expecting just to find either lumps of dandruff or clumps of my scalp coming out. But oh no, what was this? A live nit. IN MY HAIR. I hit the bottom Dearest Reader, I cannot lie to you. I was smack bang in the middle of a set of very important exams and I broke down. I was defeated. I am 22, single, jobless, in nearly £50K of debt from uni fees and now riddled with headlice. I wanted to chop all of my extremely long, thick and VERY curly hair off and never go outside ever again. But I picked myself up. Trawled through Mumsnet (a scary place for someone who has not got children. I salute you all). Found this as the most recommended weapon against them. So I used it and immediately the itching stopped. Incredible I thought, but also too easy. I have always been an easy target for them see Dearest Reader, as my thick curls provide a safe haven for them. Oh but\... it was that easy. I used it again a week after and still- nothing. Now I am using the metal comb too, I should make you aware of that. But anything I pulled out the first time I used the product was dead. I haven't pulled anything out since- despite going through it for an hour. So- one downside, but I refuse to deduct a mark because I'm so pleased to be back at 22, single, jobless and nearly £50K in debt but NIT FREE. This did leave my hair very greasy. Maybe because I used too much, maybe because I left it in for 40 minutes\... who knows. So use it over the weekend as it took me 3 washes to make sure my hair was back to its dry and grease free self. Good luck out there. And remember- someone always has it worse off than you.

title: Autism friendly product that actually works!!
review_body: Finally we are nit free! If I could give this 100 stars I would! We tried so many products that didn't work. Not only that, they were so unpleasant to use, with over powering fragrances and greasy, oily textures, all of which are sensory hell for an autistic child. So after no success and the trauma of the other treatments, I am SO RELIEVED to have finally found something that actually works!! The spray is so easy to use, the fragrance is lovely and not too strong, the texture is oily but it stays in the hair and doesn't drip making combing very easy. The comb included works well and my son much preferred it but I did use a nitty gritty comb on the first treatment too, just to be sure. However I only used the NitWits comb on the second treatment but thankfully found nothing (at last!) after a thorough comb through. Pro tip for washing out is to work the hair into a lather with shampoo BEFORE wetting it, and THEN rinse it out really well. This should prevent the hair being greasy after the treatment. Highly recommend!

title: This killed lice and nits in one wash
review_body: I used this product on me and my two kids. We were itching for a week before I realised we probably had nits, having had an email from school to say someone in the class had had them. The product arrived with the plastic comb so I combed through our hair and found two live lice in my son's hair, two in mine and a few in my other son. I applied the treatment and drenched our hair thoroughly with it. It is oily but I was careful to tell the boys not to look down and sat them in front of the tv looking straight ahead with towels everywhere. The oil can get into eyes without you feeling like it's running down your face so keep heads straight, don't look down and wide foreheads sporadically. I bought two bottles and one would have been fine. Left the treatment on for 30 mins. Rinsed, washed and combed using their plastic comb. After a week no sign of any lice but plenty of nits remained and could not be removed with the plastic comb, only a finger nail could prise them off the hair. I then purchased a metal nit comb separately and have combed out 200 odd nits between the three of us. All dead because nothing hatched within the week. I've never had nits before and so relieved that this appears to have rid of them. We all stopped itching a day or two after treatment as well. Would definitely recommend this product but not the plastic comb. Buy a metal one separately to rid of dead eggs.

title: It works if you do it correctly!
review_body: There was a head lice outbreak at my son's school. Spread to my younger son and myself too. It was not a very bad infestation though, but it was still horrible. First time ever dealing with lice so I needed to educate myself properly. Bought this product based on good reviews and that it does not contain harsh chemicals. You just need to follow the instructions!!! Apply the product that the entire head is covered, massage it in, leave it for 20 minutes and wash it out properly. I do not agree that you don't need to comb it out. You do have to! In fact, for short hair, the comb that comes with the product is good but if you have longer hair you need nitty gritty or similar comb. Then you do need to continue combing for another week and repeat the treatment in 7 days to kill any remaining eggs or hatched lice. One treatment is NOT ENOUGH. I bought two bottles and treated the 3 of us's hair. Then combed it through again and we are lice free now. But will continue to keep an eye and I now apply lice repellent spray before they go to school. You need to wash everything in 60 degrees after the first and second treatment. Seal any unwashable clothing for 2 days or longer. Hoover the entire house. I would say whoever had a bad review on this product, did not do it properly! Its not an easy thing to get rid of! Good luck.

title: So much easier than the Dragons\' Den dimethicone
review_body: equivalent Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 14 June 2024 Last time we had unwelcome visitors, I reached for the orange Dragons\' Den product. It worked, but it was quite hard work and you have to use Fairy or other dish soap equivalent to wash it out. This time I ordered NitWits based on the raving reviews and WOW. I 100% recommend it. It got rid of everything after one application. (I used it again 7 days after as a precaution, but found no lice at all.) The application is very easy, although if you do it properly, it is still fairly time consuming for those with long / thick / a lot of hair (I recommend getting a comb with the needle at the end to ensure you can separate your layers and really apply it everywhere.). I left it on for nearly two hours just to be sure it worked. When it was time to wash it off, I put shampoo straight onto it (quite a lot of shampoo) and massaged it in (do not rinse the hair first!!) and rinsed after. Second time round the shampoo lathered a lot already and that was it. All done. No washing up liquid, no need to wash and wash and rinse and rinse\... How much: I used up two bottles for three children. All very long hair. But I used quite a lot just to make sure. Second time round I used two bottles for the three and myself (quite long hair too) and still had some left (I got wiser about how it spreads through the hair by combing - although saying that, I felt better using a copious amount of the stuff the first time round). As much as I am raving about this product, I hope I never have to use it again. Ever. Haha. Note: I wouldn\'t bother using the enclosed comb. The NitNot comb is much better. I used to swear by NittyGritty, but now I am a convert

title: Best purchase yet!
review_body: My kids get head lice yearly from school. And apparently missed eggs, that somehow die and don't hatch can hatch later on causing an issue without you aware... we have the lice preventing spray, the full kit from the store, tried the shampoos, I've done 12hrs sitting and going through every strand of hair on all 3 kids all weekend. Nit combs don't always work! I've purchased a black light flashlight Ligit does not work on lice or eggs .... olive oil and mayo treatments work but take all day or night followed by greasy hair and a shower.. Past several years done it and tried it all. It's exhausting!!! Came across this product. Was really skeptical purchasing.. but the comments changed my mind after going through so many! Didn't see any bugs on my daughter but found several eggs. So I thought I'd try it on her first, used the comb after. Still didn't see any bugs. But removed another 3 eggs as I was checking through. After I found a live lice on my shirt at work a day or two prior, thought I'd go ahead and use it too.. and after the usage I washed my hair. Damp dried it, and brushed my hair over the tub, and wow... I didn't realize how bad I had it, and they were all dead! Was sooo excited with this product! Definitely will stick to this moving forward, not sure about the eggs just yet. But will most likely do some 3-5 hr olive oil treatments once a week for the next month or so on everyone to be sure any live eggs don't grow into adults. title: Good product as described

title: Effective
review_body: I can\'t believe it - I\'m 38 and I saw a headlouse in the shower after I had washed my hair! I was looking for new treatments as I worked with children about 10 years ago and treated my hair routinely just in case, therefore was worried I my hair may be resistant to other products. Admittedly I have shoulder length hair but it\'s very thick, although it was easy to apply myself. I sectioned my hair into layers to apply it and it soaked my hair very quickly. It\'s more of an oil than a water spray but it doesn\'t have a putrid smell as other treatments have. It\'s more of a mild smell of home hair colourant. It\'s only been a few days since I used it but no little friends have fallen out my hair since. However, there are some tips that I would like to mention and share to help others who are yet to use it\... There are some tips 1\) Make sure you cover your floor, especially if you are using it on a tiled floor. If any spray gets on the floor then it becomes extremely slippery. An old sheet or large towel would work. 2\) Keep the towel on your shoulders whilst it is processing, as the oil starts to drip put of your hair. 3\) The oil is HORRENDOUS to get out or your hair when rinsing it. I used a couple of washes of shower gel to cut through the oil until it was (literally) squeaky clean then continued to wash it as normal. Hairdressers would probably be shocked with that, but a leave in conditioner after the wash helps get it back to normal. So all in all, it\'s a fantastic product and is very effective.

Finalmente me decidí ir buscar un líquido de estos ya que la niña estaba molesta todo el día. Vi que esté tenía buenas opiniones en lo referente a las liendres asique lo compré. El olor es bastante fuerte y desagradable hasta el punto que le tuve que poner mascarilla a la niña hasta acabar de aplicarlo. Tras esto ya no molesta tanto cuando se le coloca el gorro de plástico que viene junto al bote en la caja. Yo recomendaría si hay opción de hacer la aplicación al aire libre ya que llega a costar respirar según vas echándolo. Por otro lado la aplicación es bastante sencilla ya que es un líquido muy fluido y basta con echar la mitad de este. En nuestro caso fue suficiente para humedecer todo el pelo como indican en las instrucciones. En las dos veces que he tenido que utilizarlo he tenido que reaplicar a la semana el otro medio bote para acabar completamente con todos los piojos y liendres como se indica en las instrucciones ya que con una aplicación no era suficiente pero con esta segunda vez es totalmente efectivo y no hay de que preocuparse.

Würde das Produkt am liebsten zurückgeben und mein Geld zurück verlangen, ist nur leider nicht möglich, was um so ärgerlicher ist. Man wird auf eine Support Seite weitergeleitet, die nur wenig hilfreich ist, da wir von der Anleitung her nichts falsch gemacht haben!!!

title: Used Once and Shocked by the Hair Fall
review_body: 1 out of 5 stars - Used Once and Shocked by the Hair Fall I am honestly very disappointed and quite worried after using this product. We applied it only once on our 2-year-old daughter, and within hours we noticed a lot of hair fall. She has never had any hair fall issues before this, not even a single hairfall, but after using this treatment just once, we found a worrying amount of hair falling out. We followed the instructions exactly as written, so this was really unexpected and stressful for us as parents. I genuinely bought this because of the good reviews, but our experience was the opposite. Maybe it works for some people, but based on what happened to our daughter, we definitely won't be using it again. I hope this helps other parents decide, especially if you have very young children. For us, it caused more panic than relief.

title: Didnt work for us
review_body: I really wanted this to work for us as i really like the concept and it being more natural but it didnt work for us. It was messy to apply and dribbles down your head. My kids hated the feel. They were already fed up enough to be doing it and this made things worse. You have to leave it on up to 2hrs. We did the full 2hrs. Again didnt help the grumpyness of my kids all the waiting but its really greasy so didnt want them getting it everywhere. We watched a film. Brushing through it to get nits out after treatment was awful, all the solution goes everywhere, we did see a few dead nits so thought it had done ok. But then we couldn\'t wash it out. Any gentleness with the solution was lost as we had to revert to washing up liquid as suggested on the instructions after 4 washes and it still leaving a greasy residue. I felt at the time it had done an ok job. I then checked the next day, still loads on nits. We didnt get this previously using headrin on a different occasion. It just worked and only took a 15min treatment. So i dry combed with conditioner and to be honest it was as though i hadnt treated the hair. I wouldnt recommend.

title: Read this before you buy.
review_body: Now to be fair I read all reviews sounded wonderful then I saw as seen on Dragons Den okay I was in. The product arrived quickly and so I prepared to use it. I read the instructions removed the silver foil blocking the dispenser and away I went. I turned the nozzle to the open position and nothing came out I had already shaken the liquid so didn\'t know why nothing was happening. Turns out when I squeezed the bottle the cover on the top was closing up . I cleaned it replaced it and tried again and again nothing so I removed the lid at which point the bottle poured out all over the me and the floor plus dogbed thick slimy liquid. Applied by hand from the bottle and had previously combed hair to silky soft no lugs. Left liquid about 45 mins then put provided nit comb through hair not much happened so I brushed it and had some success with the hair in my brush. Left lotion half hour again and couldn\'t get nit comb through my hair it was lugging so bad. For me this was a definite NO. Redeeming feature was no smell. Thoroughly disappointed and would not recommend for me and expensive option

title: Too much hair fall
review_body: I don't usually leave reviews but needed to this time. Tried this product because it's non toxic and for various hair types. My daughter who is 5 years old has very very thick curly hair. The product is very oily and gloopy and transfers on everything. For an active 5 year old it's very hard to stop her putting her head down or against anything. After leaving the treatment on for a couple hours I started going through her hair with the nit comb provided. I was traumatised by the amount of hair fall she had! I had pre-combed her hair before applying the treatment and combed her hair again before using the nit comb. The hair fall after the treatment was crazy. Unfortunately I won't be using this product again. Such a shame really as I had high hopes for it considering the reviews

title: Thick, sticky, greasy
review_body: I would not buy again. Extremely thick and greasy, ended up using the metal comb from another brand. Washing out is difficult. Will go back to buying the five minute one.

title: Waste of money
review_body: Disappointed that this product does not work at all. I\'ve been through 2 bottles of the stuff over 3 months, following the instructions precisely and it has made no impact on the lice. The comb is flimsy and doesn\'t get through the hair. The seal was faulty on both bottles, meaning I struggled to open and get the nozzle in place. Waste of money.

review_body: I left it on for longer than the 20 minutes and despite that it still didn't work. I followed their instructions and it did not work. I don't think this product kills them.

title: Nicht gut
review_body: Ich könnte das nicht mehr abspülen

title: Worst product I have ever used. Eurgh.
review_body: This product is literally like using thick syrup or tar all on your hair. Noticed lice in daughters hair so treated her and her 2 siblings just incase. They screamed from applying the product, waiting 2 hours and then right through to washing it out. Whilst it doesn\'t have a smell, it is by far easy to apply as the product doesn\'t smear nor lather. It literally sticks to where u apply it with no movement. It gets everywhere leaving grease type stains wherever the kids sat during waiting time which i am hoping will come out in a wash! Only pro was considering really just how very thick and greasy this product is, fairy liquid as it stated did cut straight through it when washing their hair!. I won\'t be purchasing to use for the post 7 day treatment, I will go for the typical well known household name products just for ease of application and comfort/messiness reasons!. 1/10 by best from me.

title: Do not recommend!
review_body: This product is 100% silicone! I caught nits at a wedding last weekend, and like in school days have had to go through the gruelling process of removal. I saw this product, and thought \'great! A one stop shop!\'. It arrived, and I applied it to my head. It comes out like a thick glue, and soon enough my whole head was like I had poured a bucket of PVA over it. I waited for half an hour and then dove into the combing process. Oh my god. Within the first inch of my head, my scalp was raw with hair getting pulled out by the thick concoction! I tried to be patient, but soon realised I should not have my head covered in this rubbish, and grabbed the fairy liquid and washed it all out. I\'ve since used the old school approach of coconut oil and a plastic bag, and a hairdryer to dry hair to burn them out. That\'s worked wonders and been half the (literal!) headache! Never again will I buy something \'dragons den\'! More fool me!

title: Pessima esperienza.
review_body: Purtroppo mi son fidata di tutte le recensioni positive ma su di me è stato un disastro. Mi ha completamente irritato e seccato il cuoio capelluto, l ho usato diversi mesi fa e ancora ne pago le conseguenze. Ho la cute squamata e non riesco a ripristinarla in nessun modo. Io che non ho mai avuto problemi di secchezza o forfora\...mai l\'avessi comprato. Sconsiglio fortemente.

review_body: Unglaublich dickfettig Eine Anwendung hat überhaupt nichts gebracht

title: Non uccide pidocchi, difficile da lavare, istruzioni
review_body: poco chiare. Reviewed in Italy on 4 September 2025 Non ha funzionato. Il Dimeticone non uccide i pidocchi ma gli soffoca. Quindi se lo usi parsimoniosamente come dice sulla etichetta secondo me rischi che non funzioni. Dicono 20 minuti di posa ma poi dicono anche 2 ore. Io lo riprovo mettendo più prodotto e lasciandolo per più di un ora per vedere se funziona. Concordo che è difficile da lavare dai capelli. title: Awful
review_body: Awful! Does not work at all, complete waste of money.

title: Posh olive oil
review_body: This is a very well marketed and overpriced product but you may as well use olive oil for its effectiveness. This doesn\'t smell or sting but it also doesn\'t really work. Leave in for 2 hours while using a nit comb? Who has time for that? I left it in overnight and used the comb and was still scratching the next day (albeit a bit less). In addition, this stuff is impossible to wash out. Dishwashing liquid- 4 washes later and my hair is still gunky. I\'m so glad that I\'m not trying to get this out of my three year old\'s hair. The chemical products kill everything in 15 minutes and then you just comb out the ones that didn\'t wash out. If you want a gentle \'eco\' version, save your money and just use olive oil overnight in conjunction with a comb. It is about the same level of efficacy. Would not recommend. Would not buy again.

title: Comb issues for thick hair
review_body: Not sure about the serum as only used once, but on 1st impressions its thick, not necessarily bad. But the smell after washing wasnt nice. I had to re-wash my daughters hair. The comb however was the mai reason for my review. Some teeth snapped on the 1st use, so not very strong at all

title: Does not kill eggs
review_body: Does not kill the eggs at all

title: Waste of effort and money. Stick to patience, a nit
review_body: comb and conditioner. Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 25 August 2021 Having watched the instructional video we applied this treatment to a family of 5\`s hair. It was done precisely, and took longer than my tried and trusted version of using conditioner and a Nitty Gritty comb. I bought this solution believing that the applications would save combing time in the long run. 10 days and two applications later we\'re still all scratching. Guess what, the conditioner and comb are gonna come back out and used instead. I\'m really disappointed. I chose this to get away from the chemically smelling treatments, but it\'s been entirely false economy. Bought two bottles, but sealed one is being sent back. I was utterly suckered into making this purchase as a fool for their clever marketing\... There\'s an obvious investment made to get it to appear at the top of search listings, and the fact that it featured on Dragon\'s Den is another clever consumer manipulation. There\'s no odour and now I wonder if it\'s no different to tipping veg oil all over your head 😩

title: Ineffective
review_body: Followed the instructions carefully...left for 2 hours, combed through and washed. Still live present. Don't waste your money

title: DIdn\'t work
review_body: After trying everything on the market, I was desperately hoping this would work. It didn\'t. I found it easy to use, and not as drippy and oily as Hedrin. Leaving it for two hours wasn\'t a problem and It wasn\'t hard to get out either. One bottle lasted for three uses. I followed the video to the letter., and even used a nit comb before rinsing it out. Unfortunately, after three applications, and still using conditioner and combing inetween, it hasn\'t worked. In fact, it seems to make the itching worse. I think it removed the nit case, and left the nymphs behind. Nothing works on super lice, and I am beginning to believe that nothing else but all-out chemical warfare will kill these things. I am waiting for Sklice to be made available in the UK.

title: Nits

review_body: This is such a terrible product in comparison to other products for the same thing! it's so thick really over oily and gloopy and u have to wash it out of your hair with fairy Iiquid as per instructions and even then i had to re-wash it again the next day also just to get rid of the oilyness! Most importantly tho IT DID NOT WORK!!!! so please don't waste your money! What were u thinking of Dragons Den!!!!

title: Doesn\'t work.
review_body: I have followed the instructions to the letter.. Unfortunately, this product has been completely unafectual.. I do however have a tip for washing it out.. I used lemon shower gel.

title: Does not work
review_body: It does not work as effectively as other reviews state. In fact it barely made any difference. It's unbelievably sticky however washing up liquid is not necessary to remove product as other review state. Just add shampoo after you have finished the treatment and slowly add water this should remove a lot of the product. This also would not be suitable for three people with shoulder length hair. I would say use a different brand not Hedrin again that's not effective. The nit comb that is recommended again do not waste your money. I am trying three different treatments three days apart and combing daily. I was given the same advice as another review to use this method. It can be expensive just shop around on the internet for deals. This is the only way to stop more eggs hatching and kill off any lice. My advice don't believe all the reviews!!

title: Don\'t use it
review_body: Instructions were very hard to read. Went on like glue, very greasy. Instructions said to use washing up liquid to wash, took 3 washes to get most of it out. Hair didn\'t dry, just stayed damp and sticky, needed to wash again and still greasy. Horrible, hair took a few days of washing each day to get rid of it Wasted a lot of time and money. Don\'t buy it, there are lots of other brands which are good quality and easier to use

title: Aiuto
review_body: In commercio ci sono prodotti decisamente più gestibili. Ne avevo provato uno in confezione viola con pettinino. Mio marito ha sbagliato e ha ricomprato questo. Già il fatto che sia olio e che le istruzioni suggeriscano un lavaggio con detersivo dei piatti per rimuoverlo bene dai capelli la dice lunga sul prodotto. Va tenuto da 20 Min a 2h (intervallo di tempo quanto meno ampio e anche lì 2h sono mai gestibili coi bambini??) .. si professa ecologico, ma con la quantità di detergente che ho usato e dovrò ancora usare per rimuovere questo impiastro, mi chiedo come si possa definire un prodotto ecologico..

title: Pessimo prodotto
review_body: Pessimo prodotto. Ho seguito le istruzioni: 2 ore in posa, effettivamente inodore, poi risciacquo con sapone per i piatti. Risultato: i capelli completamente rovinati. Un unto che non viene via e i capelli completamente sfibrati dall\'utilizzo del sapone per piatti. Mai più e mai poi. Dimenticavo: i pidocchi sempre al loro posto. Nessuna efficacia. Si salva solo il pettinino ergonomico.

title: Difficult to use, sticky, messy and not enough of it,
review_body: sorry! Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 17 January 2023 In high anticipation after reading the review, I thought to try this Dragon\'s Den choice after my daughter came home with potential nits from school. Well it was sadly quite a disappointment. Not only is it extremely sticky, oily and difficult to avoid getting stains in carpets, chairs or other, it needs to stay in for an enormous 2 hours! It is quite expensive if you have a daughter with medium/long hair. The \'serum\' is very hard to distribute and my daughter\'s hair got to tangled using it to comb it out properly. She could not manage to sit still for 2 hours with a very oily head, so we washed it out before the recommended. So off to the Boots and get something quicker and easier to use.

I am so angry!

title: Don\'t waste your money

title: Läuse werden aus meiner Sicht nur betäubt
review_body: Habe es jetzt zum Zweiten Mal angewendet. Jedes Mal fängt es am 2. Tag wieder an zu jucken. Werden die Viecher nur ohnmächtig oder warum ist das so. Update: Ich bin mir sicher. Aus meiner Sicht werden die Läuse bis zu 48 Stunden betäubt. Habe dieses Zeug 2 mal an meiner ganzen Familie ausprobiert. (insgesamt die 3. Behandlung) Beim zweiten mal noch dicker eingeschmiert und viel Zeit gelassen. Alle Klamotten und Bezüge direkt gewaschen. Trotzdem nach 1-2 Tagen wieder Läuse. Wenn man es schafft sofort alle Läuse raus zu kämmen, wird man wohl zufrieden sein. Ansonsten hat man Pech gehabt und die Läuse bleiben. Sie sterben definitiv nicht von diesen Mittel. Zudem trocknet es sehr stark die Haut aus. Wir haben jetzt eine andere Lösung gefunden. Bio ist das Zeug auch nicht, wie hier manche es beschreiben. Bitte einmal nachschlagen was Biozid bedeutet.

title: Very gloopy
review_body: I had high hopes for this but was disappointed. When I opened the bottle trying to get the seal off was tricky it then exploded and went in my eye, not a good start. The product is far too gloopy understand why to catch the bits i assume but whilst the 2 hours it is on it drips everywhere.. when i tried to comb it through with the nit comb the product just splashed all over the place and i could not get the comb through my hair. very disappointed as I and my girls have long hair and I was hoping for a better product than I usually use, Very disappointed I am afraid.

title: Doesn't work
review_body: I bought this, quite hopeful, but after using once and head itching the next day (so worse than just using conditioner), I used the rest of the bottle for a deep long treatment hoping that would def be it.... But again, it didn't stop the nit... was itchy within a day again. And I don't think I even had many of them, but they keep returning. Definitely wouldn't recommend. They shouldn't be able to make the 100% claim when that is clearly not the case. I would've thought that is an advertising standards breach tbh. I have moved to conditioner or oil and overnight and keeping my finger, toes and hairs crossed!!

title: Greasy and doesn't kill the lice
review_body: This product is the worst lice treatment I have used on my children's hair! It is so greasy and after washing it out their hair still looked so greasy. They were due a hair cut the next day and the hairdresser had to do three shampoo's to remove the product. We still found alive lice after treatment. Will definitely not buy this again.

title: Avoid - unless you want to lose two hours of your
review_body: life Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 3 November 2022 This solution needs to sit in your hair for two hours. It is thick, odourless oil, that drips down your neck, into your eyes, and into your ears for that whole time. The bottle advises to you use \"detergent\" to wash it out - which says it all! It took three washes with Fairy Liquid to strip this solution out of my 14 year old son\'s hair. He was in agony by the end of it - and his scalp is extremely sore. Avoid at all costs unless you want a miserable evening. I have no idea if it works but I\'ll not be using it again.

title: Not worth it - ruined clothes
review_body: Am surprised by the number of positive reviews for this lotion. Have children with near waist length hair and despite the repeated requests from the husband to give everyone a grade 1 - I was optimistic and decided to give this lotion a try. The consistency is like castor oil or honey and took nearly a whole bottle per child (and it has to repeated a week later to catch any eggs that have hatched). Left it on between 1 and 3 hrs due to the sheer amount of time it took to comb through the hair (not the products fault) - however, as you\'re combing through the lotion - it sprays and drips everywhere. I put shower caps on everyone while they waited for it to work and it dripped onto their clothing despite towels over their shoulders. Fortunately, I had put a plastic sheet down but as I had sliders on - I inadvertently carried in onto the tiled floor and three of us nearly slipped on it (it was in different places\...in case you wondering why we didn\'t clean up after the first near fall). I was initially pleased with the plastic comb (it\'s white, so easy to see the nits) but if there is any resistance or the hair is thick - the comb teeth bend or splay rendering it useless. My hands were covered in the solution so it continually slipped out - despite repeatedly washing my hands and using kitchen roll in between. I ended up using the Nitty Gritty comb we already had (which isn\'t that great either as you can\'t see if you\'re picking up nits properly). Finally, used washing up liquid once, followed by shampoo, which did the job. It removed most of the nits but all of the tops the children & I were wearing have been ruined by the splatter and drips. For the 7 day follow up, I will be switching to another brand - there are far easier choices without the mess, expense of the lotion (esp. as it\'s more expensive than most) - and ruined clothes to boot. Now maybe that grade 1 isn\'t such a bad idea after all..

title: Not a winner for us
review_body: Update after 2nd treatment: I followed up a week later with a second treatment. Along with more eggs I found one very large adult louse which was either newly caught or survived the original application. But seems to have worked eventually. Increased rating as it did eventually work, even if the first application wasn't a success. \- With two kids in infant school lice are constantly making their way back into our house so wish I had the positive experience so many others had, but I found this possibly the worst I've tried to date. Pros: \- No nasty smell (although found the same of Hedrin and Full Marks). \- Found it significantly easier to remove eggs when combing through with a nit comb before washing out compared to the other brands. \- Washes out as easily as any other. Did so the only way I've ever done it with any brand (fairy liquid straight onto hair without wetting, then rinse and repeat 2 more times before a final wash with shampoo). It takes 5 minutes and left the hair squeaky clean - although not as soft and conditioned-looking as other brands. Cons: \- The only one that matters. Doesn't kill the lice! I presume the claim that it kills 100% is based on the drowning lice in the stuff in a lab rather than real world conditions. I applied as directed, but left for 5 hours as I was sceptical. After 5 hours I saw two crawling around in her hair still very much alive. I then re-applied for 2 more hours and did a final go over with a nit comb. \- Awful to apply, for sure the worst of the three brands I've tried. So very oily and gloopy. I'll finish the bottles with a follow up treatment but then it'll be back to Full Marks for us.

title: There\'s a method to killing lice and it doesn\'t
review_body: really depend on the product you use Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 26 November 2022 With two kids in primary school and one in nursery, I have been battling lice for years now and multiple times per term. I am resigned to the fact that I will be treating lice at least once a month until they\'re in secondary. So this is a very costly task - both in money and time. So my review is based on those two factors because after trying literally every product on the market, not a single one is 100% effective first time. And if you\'re expecting to do a one and done treatment, you will be repeating this cycle more often than necessary because you absolutely have to break the cycle and repeat your treatment. So in my opinion, you\'re better off ignoring the effectiveness claims of a product and focus more on the time and money aspects and make sure your method is correct. PROS There are lots of pros to using this product outside of the ones shown in the listing. It is the only way to get a comb through my daughters very thick hair and I think this would be a good fit for anyone with thick, curly, or dry hair. I would never, ever, put fairy liquid on my children\'s hair. I don\'t want to deal with the damage and dryness but I can understand it\'s a cheap option for dissolving the oil. I have found that if you apply any shampoo directly to the hair BEFORE water, it creates a creamy lather. Add little bits of water to make it lather more, rinse and repeat 2 more times. This has been effective at getting it out. It is competitively priced for what\'s out there but given the cons (below) the price feels a bit high as the time required to treat is so much longer than the other options out there. CONS This is a very time consuming option compared to other products. I like that it\'s eco friendly and just one ingredient but waiting 2 hours to comb and trying to keep kids away from pillows and couches (because it transfers oil everywhere) is a stressful nightmare. Some of them will keep a towel on their heads but try doing that with my 2 year old, not a chance. Washing it out takes forever too. There are shampoo based treatments out there and other oil based options that take 20 minutes or less to be \'effective\'. None of them are 100% like I said so I tend to purchase the quicker ones and take an awful amount of care combing. I\'ve even had luck using pure coconut oil with a few drops of peppermint and tea tree oil, but the same time consuming issue applies to this option (and the oily mess) but it\'s the cheapest option I\'ve found. The MOST important thing you can do in this process is to repeat it in 7 days (and many times I\'ve done a third treatment 7 days after that and found the lice still kicking). I love all of the advice provided in the listing, all of it is spot on in my experience, so don\'t skip any of these steps and you should be lice free in no time! I would recommend this product to people with certain hair types and older children who can be responsible and stay away from textiles. If you are short on time or have younger children, you\'re better off getting a 15 minute shampoo option, combing really well, and repeating in a week. It is the same result. Hope this helps!

title: It did the trick
review_body: After trying a few other products and battling with head lice for a while I am pleased to report this did the trick. However out of all the products we tried this was certainly the messiest to apply. My daughter has waist length thick hair and one bottle was only just enough for one treatment and as two treatments are required we needed another bottle. My daughter has sensory difficulties and found having to keep this product in for two hours difficult to tolerate. After applying the solution we wrapped her hair in a plastic bag to stop it dripping everywhere. Be very careful not to get it on tile or laminate floor - despite cleaning several times we are still slipping all over the place! It did take a bit of time to wash out of hair but it did come out with washing up liquid as advised on the packaging. Overall I would use it again if needed but wish it wasn't so messy.

review_body: ingredients Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 10 July 2021 Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) Last week my daughter came home with the dreaded note from her teacher; her class has a nit infestation. I'd already gone through her hair with no sign of head lice but when I saw this, I loved the idea and ordered straight away. You can't be too thorough when it comes to these things and the stress it can bring the household is not worth thinking about. The bottle contains 200ml which is more than most of the leading brands. It also has more of the key nit killing ingredient without the unhealthy addition of alcohol and solvents. The issue with those products is they can dry out the scalp and aren't always effective on the nits. This is a clear liquid with no strong scent. It calls itself a serum, and it really is. The consistency is very slick but quite thick. The product is easy to spread over the whole of the scalp and hair. It goes a long way, so you I could easily treat my daughters long hair multiple times with one bottle. The treatment makes it easy to comb through my daughters hair. The comb slides straight through. It doesn't foam up either, so you can see if there are any nits on the comb. You do have to leave it on for two hours (I put a shower cap on my daughter to keep it contained). Because of the serums consistency, i put clarifying shampoo straight onto her hair after treatment and before water just to help it wash out. I shampooed 3 times in total and rinsed really well. The result is her hairs In good condition and there are no nits so I'm very pleased.

title: Of all products around this one stands out
review_body: Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) I am hugely impressed by the web page for this product with the easy to follow instructions and helpful questions and answers. No unrealistic claims are made and clear explanations are made about the life cycle and appearance of these depressingly common and indiscriminate pests. As products go this potion has the highest credentials and the best of ingredients that won't cause harm or eco damage. Well done. It was good have this ready for the next inevitable infestation at the beginning of this summer's holidays. No scent at all so not offensive to use, the thick gel sticks to all parts of the hair and scalp to do its work. After the two hours and two shampoo Ingatestone first with washing up liquid as advised, all the nits were dead and combed out. Next morning check there were none at all to be found so off we went to the barber confident that we wouldn't be turned away. As a side note I would say that my grandson was really grateful to get this sorted as he admitted to being most embarrassed by his nit infestation let alone being upset about not being able to sleep for the itching. Bring back the nit nurse please NHS and recommend everyone to use this great product!

title: It works
review_body: Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product( What\'s this? ) This is a very effective nit treatment solution with the added benefit of being eco friendly. It doesnt have an overpowering chemically smell like many do. It is easy to apply and comb through using a nit comb. It also has a long shelflife so is great for keeping in the cupboard for future use. A couple things that arent so great are: the consistency is incredibly thick so even though it does work effectively, it does take some time and addtional products to wash out. Also the wait time after application is 2 hours which is a long time, especially if your plan is to use it prior to normal bath time before bed etc. Otherwise it performs well so would recommend. title: Le produit MIRACLE
review_body: Je dois le dire, j\'ai lutté pendant des années contre les poux. Des années où j\'ai tout, TOUT essayé (sauf raser la tête de mes filles\...). J\'ai essayé les produits de parapharmacie, les marques bien connues, qui coûtent un bras avec traitement que tu recommences x fois pour des récidives assurées, j\'ai essayé les méthodes artisanales genre masque à l\'huile d\'olive et à la mayo, j\'ai même testé le henné neutre (recommandation d\'une coiffeuse qui m\'avait dit que c\'était radical\... mais bon, ça a juste donné un max de brillance et de souplesse aux cheveux de mes filles, les poux eux, n\'ont même pas été égratignés). J\'ai donc jeté beaucoup d\'argent par les fenêtres, tellement que je me demande si avec tous l\'argent perdu dans ces produits inefficaces, on n\'aurait pas pu se payer un beau week-end en famille. Dans mes errances sur Amazon, alors que je m\'apprêtais à commander deux tubes de baume décolleur de lentes (oui parce qu\'en plus des poux, les lentes mortes inesthétiques restent soudées aux cheveux comme s\'il fallait garder des stigmates de cette guerre sans fin ! Genre \"vous avez gagné cette bataille mais pas la guerre\" vous voyez\...) quand je suis tombée sur les commentaires à propos de ce produit anti-poux Nit Not. C\'était sans appel : c\'est THE produit miracle qui te débarrasse des poux à coup sûr. Je me suis dit que ça valait le coup de tenter même si, de prime abord, le produit me semblait un peu cher. J\'ai commandé, j\'ai testé et je n\'en ai pas cru mes yeux. Infestation sur deux têtes à longs cheveux, le flacon m\'a permis de faire 2 applications sur l\'une et 3 sur l\'autre (ses cheveux sont plus fins). Après ce traitement, plus un seul poux pendant des mois ! Puis réapparition sur une tête des mois après, traitement en une seule application, terminé, adios les poux. C\'est difficile à croire quand on a tout essayé mais ce produit, c\'est juste dingue comme il fonctionne bien. Il est effectivement TRES gras mais il s\'applique très facilement. Il faut juste prévoir des serviettes pour protéger un peu la zone. Rinçage : prévoyez plusieurs shampoing mais ça part assez facilement malgré tout. Résultats : cheveux doux et brillants, pas d\'odeur caractéristique, plus de poux. Reste à retirer les lentes mortes qui ont résisté au peigne et basta. Au final, le produit qui me semblait cher ne l\'est pas du tout car m\'a permis de faire 5 traitements et surtout sans récidive. Là où je dépensais des sommes folles tous les mois en produits nuls à la pharmacie. Vraiment, je recommande à 10000000 % Et s\'il n\'est plus dispo sur Amazon France, on le trouve aussi sur Amazon UK (et perso je suis prête à le commander à l\'étranger si besoin vu que c\'est le seul produit que j\'ai testé qui marche VRAIMENT).

title: Adieu les poux et lentes !
review_body: Le Sérum NitNot est un produit exceptionnel pour éliminer les poux et les lentes, même s\'il nécessite un investissement de temps et d\'argent. Voici mon avis détaillé : Points forts 1\. Efficacité exceptionnelle: Le Sérum NitNot est incroyablement efficace, même sur les lentes les plus tenaces. Dès la première utilisation, j\'ai remarqué une réduction significative des poux et des lentes. Après avoir suivi scrupuleusement le traitement, les cheveux de mon enfant étaient complètement débarrassés des parasites, et il ne se gratte plus du tout. 2\. Traitement minutieux mais payant : Bien que le processus soit long, il en vaut la peine. Il faut appliquer le sérum mèche par mèche, laisser agir pendant 20 à 30 minutes, puis peigner chaque mèche avec soin. J\'ai utilisé le peigne NitNot Special pour cheveux fins (à double peignes, fin et ultra-fin), ce qui a grandement facilité l\'élimination des lentes. 3\. Peignage méthodique : Après avoir laissé agir le sérum, il est essentiel de passer le peigne mèche par mèche jusqu\'à ce que le peigne soit propre à chaque passage (je rinçais le peigne sous l\'eau après chaque passage). Cette méthode méticuleuse assure qu\'aucun poux et aucune lente ne soit laissée derrière. 4\. Facilité d\'élimination des lentes : Grâce à la texture du sérum, similaire à celle du miel et assez grasse, les lentes se détachent facilement lors du peignage. Même après avoir séché les cheveux à la serviette, un dernier passage du peigne ultra-fin a permis d\'enlever les quelques lentes restantes. Considérations \- Prix élevé : Le sérum NitNot est un investissement. Cependant, vu son efficacité, je considère que cela en vaut largement la peine. \- Temps de traitement : Le processus est long et demande de la patience. Appliquer le sérum, laisser agir, peigner, faire deux shampoings, et peigner encore une fois peut sembler fastidieux. Mais le résultat final est bluffant. Conclusion Je recommande vivement le Sérum NitNot malgré le temps et le coût. La satisfaction de voir les cheveux de votre enfant complètement débarrassés de ces parasites et de constater qu\'il ne se gratte plus du tout est inestimable. Si vous êtes prêt à investir du temps pour un résultat impeccable, ce sérum est la solution idéale pour lutter contre les poux et les lentes.

title: Effective would recommend - long thick hair
review_body: Working in a children\'s home, I have caught the dreaded nits a couple times now. I haven\'t had them since I was a child and am now in my 30\'s! I had been using the Hendrin shampoo treatment, which did seem to work. But when I got them again, I thought I\'d try this. It\'s a really thick greasy serum that coats all of your hair easily. I used half the bottle on my long thick hair and will use the rest on the second treatment in 7-9 days as recommended. It was super easy to comb through whilst the treatment was in the hair and everything coming out was definitely dead, after having the treatment on for about 30 mins. I only found 3/4 lice, but a lot of eggs. I then left the treatment in for a further 1.5 hours and washed out. Whilst it recommends using washing up liquid, I used shower gel and did 3 washes and then a good conditioner. I followed up with a final comb through and there was nothing coming out on the comb! So impressed so far. I would recommend purchasing the NotNit comb too as it\'s design makes it easier to use when your hands are slippery from the treatment. The treatment doesn\'t smell of anything either, which was a bonus!

title: Amazing!
review_body: It\'s been a long time since I had to deal with headlice and all I remembered is the awful experiences with other products so it filled me with dread trying to find a product that works the first time then I came across nitnot. The product is a bit messy as it\'s oil but nothing a towel on the floor and on the shoulders can\'t fix. I left the product on for longer than 2 hours due to nap time (I placed a towl turban on my child) then I used washing up liquid first then a second wash of normal kids shampoo and all the product was gone so just conditioned as normal. There were quite a few dead louse floating in the bath water so that was the first sign this product actually worked. After that I doused my child\'s hair in leave in conditioner and thoroughly combed with the included comb to remove any dead lice still clinging on - NOTE there was no remaining alive lice after the first treatment. This is not the end of the journey as we will repeat in a weeks time but there was still the task to get rid of those little bead looking things that are nit eggs, I cannot say if the product killed the eggs but I\'m not taking any risks of them hatching in days to come- the included comb does not remove the eggs. The best method I have found is to pull them down the hair shaft with your fingernails. (I have ordered a long toothed coiled headlice comb from amazon as this is supposed to remove eggs and save time) I highly recommend this product and will keep a back up bottle at home incase we have the dreaded things invade again.

title: This will kill the live even if you can't initially
review_body: see them! Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 16 February 2025 This is exceptionally good. We have managed to dodge the primary school headlice for years until recently. Initially I treated both my girls with the Headrin 5 minute treatment. I thought given the time span was so short it was a win win, and even managed to find one louse in the process (although it was still alive). I treated her twice and she seemed fine although I only ever discovered two lice! Two weeks later, my youngest was scratching a bit and I had bought NitNot as a product-to-keep-on-hand. Prior to treatment I searched and searched her head for signs of lice and nothing. A few suspect nits but none made a popping noise when crushed. Left the treatment in for 40 minutes and lo and behold...20 lice combed out and all dead. The comb that came with it is surprisingly good although one tooth bent after a few goes. I have the nittygritty comb as my go to. What surprised me with this product was its effectiveness to get even the baby lice out. I managed to treat two girls and myself and still have 3/4 left of the bottle, so initially it seems pricy but works out to be good value for money. It's a very good product, and definitely one to keep in the cupboard for those emergencies!

title: Works amazingly first time!
review_body: I\'ve been battling headlice in my household for months. I felt like I\'d tried everything. I\'d soaked our pillow cases in boiling water, all headlice treatment brands instores, buying all new pillows, duvets and bedding, nothing worked. I was quite desperate, found this on amazon, was a littlw sceptical but all the other reviews raved on about it so I caved. You could literally see it working. When I applied this to my partners hair I went through it before hand and saw a couple, applied the nitnot and by the time I\'d done his head, I went back to check them out of curiosity and it was already dead. Once it was washed out I was going through his hair with the comb, which is the best hair comb I\'ve come across so far, but out of 3 people only 2 actual nits came out of our hairs from the comb, the rest was eggs, seemingly all the headlice had fallen out in the shower. Brilliant stuff! Only negative I can say about this is, at least for me, the texture and feeling of putting this on my hair gave me some Sensory issues, felt as though I was putting cooking oil on my hair and I couldn\'t stand the stiffness of my hair, just food for thought if you have an autistic of Sensory sensitive child. Will definitely purchase again if/when needed.

title: Headlice Gone in No Time!
review_body: This worked really well for us. The first treatment cleared about 90%, and the second one a week later got rid of the rest with no problems. The included comb was really handy, and the kids didn't mind the application or removal at all. One tip --- using shower gel first, then shampooing twice, made the treatment wash out much easier. Just shampoo alone didn't work as well on the first child, but the shower-gel method was perfect on my daughter. Reliable, easy to use, and it actually works. We'd definitely recommend it.

title: Funziona ma attenersi alle istruzioni
review_body: Funziona perfettamente, come scritto nelle istruzioni sul retro, il tempo minimo di posa è 20, ma si consiglia una posa di 2 ore per una disinfestazione totale. Consiglio di applicarlo bene senza risparmiare ( un olio molto denso e grasso) su cute e lunghezze, poi mettere una cuffia usa e getta perche dopo un po tende a colare. La confezione mi è stata sufficiente per me (capelli lunghi a metà schiena) mia figlia (media lunghezza) e mio figlio. Ne ho comprate 2 perche è fondamentale ripetere il trattamento dopo 7/9 giorni per debellare anche le uova, altrimenti vi ritrovate di nuovo con i pidocchi dopo poco tempo. Solo in casi estramamente radicati, si consiglia di ripetere il trattamento una terza volta. Il lavaggio è stato effettuato con winnis piatti (2 lavaggi, poi shampo abituale) è l\'unico modo per togliere l\'unto perche è piu denso dell\'olio extravergine di oliva.

title: The Best Product I Have Ever Used!
review_body: I received the headlice treatment within 24 hours of ordering it which I was very happy about. It does not come in excessive packaging, its in a bottle and the instructions are clearly written on it. This was another plus for me because if there is one thing I can't stand, it's having to get rid of packaging that wasn't necessary in the first place. Anyway, with that being said, I purchased this product because from what I have read it was made by a mother who got fed up with fighting a loosing battle with nits. I can totally relate to this, I have tried every product under the sun and the little buggers just keep coming back. Not only do they keep coming back, but they do so within weeks of me getting rid of them. When I say I have tried every product, I mean I have tried every product! A friend of mine has been using it on her daughter for a few months and she said it got rid of the problem completely, so I decided to give it a try. I ordered it immediately, and so far, I have only used one application, but I am very impressed. I don't usually experience such quick results after using a headlice product for the first time, but this one has surprised me. Not only has my head stopped itching, but there is a noticeable difference in the texture of my hair, it is softer and silkier than ever which is a major bonus! However, that's not what I am concerned about, I want these nits gone once and for all. It also smells pretty decent, most products smell like medicine, this one doesn't, it has a very neutral smell. With other products, I've literally had to hold my breath while I was using it, not with this one. Also, the application is very simple, when you unscrew the lid, there is a small cup inside the hole to keep the liquid in the bottle, pull that out (which is really easy to remove by the way), and then screw the lid back on. It's like a kind of squeezy bottle thing, so you've got to unscrew another small knob on the top so that you can pour it out, if that makes sense. I've probably made it sound more complicated than it is, but trust me, it's really simple! So, my first application was a massive success, the instructions state that you need to do a second application 7-10 days after the first one and that should get rid of nits for good and break the lifecycle. I don't think I've ever been so excited to use a nit treatment for the second time! LOL! They also have a website which I had a look at, it's actually very impressive, the founder of the product has a clinic in Hemel Hemstead which I think I may go and visit and get some additional advice on how to eliminate this dreaded problem for good! Very impressed, highly recommended!

title: DO NOT USE ANYTHING ELSE!! USE THIS!!
review_body: My daughter has quite a few allergies and other treatments have left her with a red, sore and even itchier scalp! I tried using just conditioner but her hair is super thick and super long and the infestation was just getting worse! I spotted this and saw that it was hypoallergenic... what more did I have to lose? I got the Piriton on standby and set to work! Instantly I knew this wasn't like any other treatment I'd tried! It really is a serum! Felt very similar to the smoothing serum I use to control my frizz. And definitely no smell! I was in love! The nozzle makes is super easy to apply and once applied just looks like you have gel in your hair (not an oily mess like some others!). There was a pretty bad infestation in my 8 year olds hair and it got rid of them all after 2 treatments (although I'm rechecking nightly just in case!). And what's better is that she had no reaction!!!!! Hooray! Now normally I buy 2-3 bottles for 1 treatment between my girls then have to buy more for the second treatment. This bottle did not only their hair for 2 treatments but mine own as well! And I probably have enough left to treat myself again if I needed too. Note - my girls both have 18 inch hair (root to tip) and THICK! Mine is much shorter (8ish inches) and I have shaved sides and back! Just wish I could treat my twins with it but both currently under 2 so don't want to risk their sensitive scalps! I washed out with store brand washing up liquid, followed by two washes with their regular shampoo and a good condition with their regular conditioners. However I forgot to bring the washing up liquid in with mine the first time so just washed twice with my normal shampoo and it came out just as well (but remember I'm the one with the short hair!). Recommending to everyone now!
